architecture.summary: A decoder-only Transformer extended with approximate kNN-augmented attention in one layer near the top of the stack. During inference, a non-differentiable external memory stores (key, value) pairs from recent tokens across documents. For each query token, the model performs both local dense self-attention (over a sliding 512-token window) and approximate kNN lookup into the external memory (retrieving top-k keys/values). The two attention outputs are combined via a learned per-head gate. Position bias is applied only to local attention; retrieved memories receive no position encoding. Memory is cleared at document boundaries. Training uses Transformer-XL style caching of keys/values from the previous step.

claims.statement: adding memory of size 8192 improves perplexity on C4(4K+) from 17.20 to 14.42 for vanilla Transformer with context size 512
performance steadily improves when increasing memory size up to 262K tokens
a Memorizing Transformer with 8k tokens in memory matches the perplexity of a vanilla Transformer with 5X more trainable parameters
step time increased from 0.2s to 0.25s with 8K memory and to 0.6s with 65K memory for a 200M parameter model
the model is capable of making use of newly defined functions and theorems during test time
the model retrieved function and variable names most frequently when accessing external memory
in 8 out of 10 manually checked examples on Isabelle, the model found the body of the lemma it needed to predict

a pretrained 1B vanilla Transformer can be finetuned to use external memory; within 20K finetuning steps it closes half the gap to a 1B Memorizing Transformer, and closes the gap entirely after 100K steps
